name: Low-Fat Flan
recipeIngredient: 14 oz Can Non-Fat sweetened Condensed Milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. In a small saucepan, combine sugar with 1/4 cup water.
3. Bring mixture to a simmer over low heat while stirring slowly.
4. Increase heat to medium-high and continue cooking without stirring,
until the syrup turns a deep amber color. Do not allow it to darken
too much or the syrup will taste burned.
5. Swirl the pan if the syrup is coloring unevenly. Immediately pour
the syrup into a 1 1/2 qt. souffle dish or casserole dish and carefully
tilt the dish so that the caramel syrup coats halfway up the side of
the dish as well as the bottom.
6. In a large bowl, whisk together eggs and egg whites. Add condensed milk,
skim milk, and vanilla, blending well.
7. Pour the mixture through a fine strainer into the caramel-coated dish.
8. Set the dish in a larger shallow pan, such as a roasting pan.
9. Pour enough hot water into the larger pan so it comes halfway up the side
of the custard dish.
10. Bake for 60-70 minutes, or until the custard is set around the edges but
still wobbly in the center.
11. Remove the dish carefully from its water bath to a rack to cool to room
temperature. Then cover with plastic wrap (not foil) and refrigerate for at least
4 hours or overnight.
To serve: Loosen flan around the edges with a knife or spatula, invert the flan
onto a serving plate.
